channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Delays with Code Scanning and Billing
item.description: Jun 1, 19:27 UTC Update - We are continuing to investigate delayed billing updates, on GitHub.com. We have applied additional mitigations are continuing to see signs of improvement, and are continuing to work to improve the processing rate. We will continue to keep users updated on progress towards...

item.title: Degraded performance for Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Jun 1, 19:06 UTC Resolved - This issue has been resolved. Jun 1, 18:28 UTC Investigating - We are investigating elevated errors on requests to Claude Sonnet 4.6. We will provide an update as soon as possible.

item.title: WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites
item.description: The security defect (CVE-2026-8732) allows unauthenticated attackers to create administrative accounts on the affected installations. The post WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cloudflare Dashboard and Cloudflare API service issues
item.description: Jun 1, 17:48 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Cloudflare APIs that are specifically impacting OAuth/Wrangler API calls. These issues do not affect the serving of cached files via the Cloudflare CDN or other security features at the Cloudflare Edge. Customers using the...

item.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
item.description: A new Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack campaign, codenamed Miasma, has compromised @redhat-cloud-services packages to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines and deliver a self-propagating worm. "This is effectively a Mini Shai-Hulud campaign: it uses the same core tactics of...

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting...

item.title: Decreased ChatGPT availability for Free users
item.description: Status: ResolvedThe issue is resolved.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Introducing Mellum2: A 12B Mixture-of-Experts Model by JetBrains
Why Video Agent models are next — Ethan He, xAI Grok Imagine
item.description: Inside xAI: Building Grok Imagine in 3 Months, Videogen vs World Models, and why Grok Imagine is so underrated. For the first time, we do a deep dive with the guy who led it!

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Jun 1, 15:41 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 1, 15:21 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 1, 14:40 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: New Linux Flaw, PAN-OS Exploit, AI-Powered Attacks, OAuth Phishing and More
item.description: Monday hit like a cron job with anger issues. A busted auth path here, a repo-side faceplant there, some "patched-ish" thing already getting chewed on in the wild, and then the usual bonus round: poisoned dev tools, sketchy forum chatter, phishing kits pretending to be productivity, and AI lowering...

item.title: Beyond LLMs: Why Scalable Enterprise AI Adoption Depends on Agent Logic
Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: Jun 1, 13:33 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 1, 12:48 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 1, 12:19 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 1, 12:15 UTC Investigating - We are currently...

item.title: Open and closed models are on different exponentials
item.description: Where marginally higher intelligence drives value, and where it doesn't.

item.title: Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus
item.description: Dragos said customers will soon gain expanded asset visibility and integrated device intelligence, with automated remediation workflows and a unified platform experience to follow. The post Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: China’s brain implant ambitions
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. China has approved the world’s first invasive brain-computer chip—here’s what’s next Sitting in the courtyard of his house in China’s Henan province...

item.title: China’s AI Stack Is No Longer Catching Up — It’s Setting the Pace
item.description: For years, the narrative around China’s AI industry was framed as a race to close the gap with the West — faster chips, bigger models, more data. But a quiet shift has occurred. Driven by Huawei’s innovative “cluster + SuperPoD” architecture and the open‑source CANN framework, a complete end‑to‑end...

item.title: As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution
item.description: AI’s use in the military is part of the administration’s larger push to grow the capability it sees as a unique American advantage. The post As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Security Growth Platform: Why MSPs Are Moving Beyond vCISO Tools
item.description: Three years ago, the practical question for an MSP building a cybersecurity practice was which "vCISO platform" to buy. The term was good shorthand for the work at the time: assessments, advisory, reporting, maybe a compliance module bolted on the side. The work has since outgrown the descriptor. A...

item.title: 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access
item.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has been released for the CIFSwitch flaw, which allows low-privileged users to escalate to root on vulnerable Linux systems. The post 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks
item.description: Hackers began exploiting CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS, four days after public disclosure. The post Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new malicious supply chain campaign that's targeting developers using OpenAI Codex through a legitimate-looking remote web UI. The tool, named codexui-android, is advertised on GitHub and npm as a remote web UI for OpenAI Codex, attracting over...

item.title: Sonnet 4.5 elevated errors
item.description: Jun 1, 09:28 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 1, 09:17 UTC Update - We are continuing to monitor for any further issues. Jun 1, 09:13 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 1, 09:08 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating...

item.title: China has approved the world’s first invasive brain-computer chip—here’s what’s next
item.description: One day last October, sitting in the courtyard of his house in China’s Henan province, Dong Hui decided to see if he could hold a pen to write.  Dong, 39, had sustained spinal cord injuries in a car accident six years earlier that left him paralyzed from the neck down. Slowly but determinedly,...

item.title: Critical WP Maps Pro Flaw Actively Exploited to Create Admin Accounts
item.description: Threat actors are attempting to actively exploit a critical security flaw impacting WP Maps Pro, a WordPress plugin that has had over 15,000 sales on the Envato Market, to create malicious administrator accounts on susceptible sites. WP Maps Pro allows site owners to embed customizable Google Maps...

item.title: JD.com Unveils 'Wolf Pack' Robot Matrix at World Intelligent Industry Expo
item.description: JD.com showcased its comprehensive "Wolf Pack" robot matrix at the 2026 World Intelligent Industry Expo, covering aerial, ground, and warehouse scenarios, while founder Liu Qiangdong pledged not to lay off any workers displaced by automation.

item.title: MiniMax Launches M3 Model With 1M Context and Native Multimodal Capabilities
item.description: MiniMax released its M3 flagship model, claiming it as the first domestic AI model to combine frontier coding, agentic capabilities, 1M-token context windows, and native multimodal processing in a single architecture.

item.title: Dreame Technology Opens Pre-IPO Funding Round at $9.6B Valuation
item.description: Dreame Technology has opened a Pre-IPO funding round with a pre-money valuation of approximately 70 billion yuan ($9.6 billion), requiring a minimum investment of 350 million yuan per investor.

item.title: OmAI Launches OttoBox AI Video Creation Assistant at BEYOND Expo 2026
item.description: OmAI unveiled OttoBox, an AI-native video creation assistant powered by its in-house OmModel multimodal model, reducing rough cut editing from 8 hours to 30 minutes.

item.title: Opus 4.7 elevated errors
item.description: Jun 1, 07:22 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 1, 07:08 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 1, 06:48 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Welcome NVIDIA Cosmos 3: The First Open Omni-model for Physical AI Reasoning and Action
PhyDrawGen: Physically Grounded Diagram Generation from Natural Language
item.description: arXiv:2605.30512v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generating physics diagrams from text requires strict adherence to physical laws. While current generative models produce visually plausible outputs, they systematically hallucinate force vectors, ignore conservation laws, and violate geometric...

item.title: Physically Viable World Models: A Case for Query-Conditioned Embodied AI
item.description: arXiv:2605.30542v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: World models for embodied AI must be physically viable: constructed to answer intervention queries by representing the physical structure governing action outcomes, rather than merely predicting future observations. Existing observation-predictive...

item.title: Transforming and Encoding FTS for SAT Solving: What Helps, What Hurts (Extended Version)
item.description: arXiv:2605.30563v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Factored tasks are a classical planning representation that extends SAS+ with limited forms of disjunctive preconditions, conditional effects, and angelic nondeterminism. This allows for a more compact representation of tasks than traditional...

item.title: Procedural Generation of First Person Shooter Maps using Map-Elites
item.description: arXiv:2605.30570v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We investigate the application of MAP-Elites (a well-known quality diversity algorithm) to design levels for First-Person Shooter (FPS) games. We consider two well-known map representations (All-Black and Grid-Graph) and introduce two novel...

item.title: Uncertainty-Aware and Temporally Regulated Expert Advice in Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Driving
item.description: arXiv:2605.30576v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Exploration in reinforcement learning for autonomous driving is inherently unsafe: agents must experience novel behaviors to learn, yet exploration can lead to collisions or off-road driving. We propose an uncertainty-aware framework that leverages...

item.title: QASM-Eval: A Dataset to Train and Evaluate LLMs on OpenQASM-3 Beyond Quantum Circuits
item.description: arXiv:2605.30358v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantum computing remains in the Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) era, where the performance is highly constrained to noise. Addressing the limitation often requires hardware-facing capabilities beyond gate-sequence circuit specification,...

item.title: Gait2Hip-60: A Unified Deep Learning Benchmark for Predicting Hip Muscle Forces and Joint Moments from Multi-Cadence Gait Kinematics
item.description: arXiv:2605.30374v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Estimating hip muscle forces and joint moments during gait typically relies on musculoskeletal simulation, which is informative but time-consuming and difficult to apply in clinical settings. This study developed a deep learning framework to predict...

item.title: Unicorn: Scaling High-Dimensional Time Series Forecasting via Universal Correlation Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2605.30376v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern time series architectures face a fundamental trade-off: channel-independent models scale well with increasing data volume but ignore critical inter-channel dependencies, while channel-dependent models are expressive but remain...

item.title: When LLMs Learn to Be Consistently Wrong: A Multi-Model Study of Linear Representations of Synthetic Deception
item.description: arXiv:2605.30381v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deceptive alignment, in which models maintain accurate internal representations while deliberately producing false outputs, remains a central challenge in AI safety. While strategic deception is the primary long-term concern, synthetic dishonesty -...

item.title: LLMs Without Deep Neural Networks: New Architecture, Benefits and Case Study
item.description: arXiv:2605.30385v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The purpose of this article is to provide validation to my deep neural network alternative in the context of LLMs. Very recently, there has been a significant interest by Chinese researchers in a model called RBF network, as a substitute to standard...

item.title: Protocol for evaluating ChatGPT in biomedical association generation and verification using a RAG-enabled, cross-model majority voting workflow
item.description: arXiv:2605.30400v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a protocol to evaluate ChatGPT's ability to generate disease-centric biomedical associations. It outlines how we generate the associations, validate the biological entities using biomedical ontologies, and verify associations using...

item.title: Exploring Autonomous Agentic Data Engineering for Model Specialization
item.description: arXiv:2605.30407v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong performance on general tasks, while often struggling to adapt to specialized domains without high-quality domain-specific data. Existing LLM-based data curation methods primarily rely on...

item.title: Domain Adaptation and Reasoning Frameworks in Language Models: A Controlled Experiment with Historical Cosmology
item.description: arXiv:2605.30415v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We investigate how domain adaptation reshapes explanatory behavior in language models using historical cosmology as a controlled setting. In Phase 1, we train a small language model from scratch on a pre-Copernican corpus from which explicit...

item.title: Cross-Lingual Steering for Figurative Language Generation
item.description: arXiv:2605.30443v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multilingual large language models can generate figurative language, but whether the internal signals driving this behavior are language-specific or reusable across languages is unclear. Using activation steering as a probe, we estimate a direction...

item.title: Can LLM Teams Play What? Where? When?
item.description: arXiv:2605.30459v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) remain limited on tasks requiring indirect reasoning, cultural knowledge, and coordinated hypothesis testing. We investigate whether team-based interaction improves LLM performance in What? Where? When? (ChGK), a quiz game...

item.title: Durable Objects and Log Explorer issues
item.description: May 31, 07:30 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. May 31, 07:28 UTC Update - Log Explorer is experiencing intermittent delays with recent logs showing up in queries. May 31, 06:42 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is aware of, and investigating an issue with...

item.title: Issues with TLS certificates using Lets Encrypt CA
item.description: May 31, 03:16 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Let's Encrypt certificates. Some visitors may experience TLS connectivity issues due to potentially unsupported CA bundling for a subset of Cloudflare customers.Customers are advised to change to a different Certificate...

item.title: Custom Hostname API issue
item.description: May 31, 01:06 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating increased latency for customers using the Cloudflare for SaaS Custom Hostname API. Customers may experience slow responses while adding a new R2 subscription.Existing Custom Hostnames remain unaffected and are operating normally.

item.title: Increased errors when accessing Workers secrets
item.description: May 30, 14:52 UTC Update - We are continuing to investigate this issue. May 30, 14:51 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare Secrets Store users may see increased errors when accessing secrets
